title: Doc grading respects store jurisdiction: a store only grades what it can see; private staleness never leaks into committed surfaces
context: Audit of the adoption pipeline for private-overlay leaks. Found one semantic gap: a doc pinned to a superseded decision whose ONLY successor lives in the private overlay would have graded 'grounded' publicly (wrong) — and any alternative that graded it 'stale' publicly would leak the EXISTENCE of a private successor into committed pages.
decision: docscan grading is jurisdiction-scoped, mirroring drift's explicit-pin-plus-live-successor rule: a pin to a superseded decision with NO successor visible in the grading store neither grounds nor stales the doc — the store cannot vouch either way, so the doc grades UNVERIFIED there. Consequences for the leak surface: staleness caused only by a private successor is invisible to the public home (doc unverified, NOT adopted, no committed copy, ledger shows ◻), while the private home grades it stale, adopts it into the overlay wiki, and heals the copy with the private current decision. Every committed artifact (component pages, adopted copies, specs ledger, CLAUDE.md pointer) is built exclusively from public-store reads via the WikiHome source gate; adopted copies are healed with the home's own decision set (status.decisions). Locked by leak tests: public home adopts nothing and prints no private id/text when the successor is private; private home re-pins to the private decision in the overlay only.
consequences: The same doc can legitimately grade unverified publicly and stale privately — maintainers with the overlay see (and heal) more than CI does, by design
Local `hunch drift` output on an overlay-configured machine names private-home page paths and doc rels (labels, never record text) — don't paste raw drift output publicly
Private decision ids appearing in committed ORIGINAL docs are user-authored content; the wiki never derives or adds ids the public store cannot see
alternatives_rejected: Grading superseded-no-successor pins as 'grounded': publicly vouches for prose the graph knows is outdated somewhere
Grading them as 'stale' publicly: the committed ledger would reveal that a hidden successor exists — existence of private memory is itself information
Union-grading for the public home with redacted rendering: redaction-miss risk; a clean public-only read at the source is provably complete (same rationale as dec_9c4289a4bb)
